evals.prompt: Create an IEEE-style campus network figure with core, distribution, and access layers in grayscale.
evals.expected_output: A diagram with academic (grayscale) theme showing three network layers in IEEE-compatible styling.
assertions: Output uses academic theme with grayscale palette
Contains core, distribution, and access layer modules
SVG or drawio output is grayscale-safe (no color-only semantics)
Node labels are <= 14 characters
meta.profile is set to academic-paper

evals.prompt: Create a research workflow figure for a paper showing data collection, preprocessing, modeling, validation, and reporting.
evals.expected_output: An academic-themed research pipeline diagram with 5 sequential stages.
assertions: Output uses academic or academic-color theme
Contains all 5 workflow stages as nodes
Edges use primary connector type for main flow
SVG export is grayscale-safe
meta.profile is set to academic-paper

evals.prompt: Create a paper-ready system architecture overview with client, API, worker, and storage layers.
evals.expected_output: An academic-themed system architecture with 4 layers suitable for publication.
assertions: Output uses academic or academic-color theme
Contains client, API, worker, and storage layers
Nodes use semantic types (service, database, user)
SVG export preserves readability in grayscale
meta.profile is set to academic-paper

evals.prompt: Create an ablation-study figure comparing baseline and two variants before a shared evaluation block.
evals.expected_output: A diagram with branching paths from baseline/variants converging at an evaluation node.
assertions: Output uses academic or academic-color theme
Contains baseline and at least two variant branches
Branches converge at a shared evaluation block
SVG export is grayscale-safe
meta.profile is set to academic-paper

evals.prompt: Create a cloud reference architecture using gateway, compute, async queue, and object storage.
evals.expected_output: A tech-blue themed cloud architecture diagram with 4 key infrastructure components.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue or similar engineering theme
Contains gateway, compute, queue, and storage nodes
Nodes use appropriate semantic types (service, queue, database)
Edge routing follows orthogonal style
Edge quality: no overlapping arrows, minimum 30px last segment
The diagram can be produced from the offline bundle workflow without requiring a live session

evals.prompt: Create an AWS reference architecture with API Gateway, Lambda, SQS, and DynamoDB. Use exact provider icons if a shape catalog is available; otherwise fall back to documented icons or semantic shapes without failing.
evals.expected_output: A valid architecture diagram that prefers AWS icons when possible but still succeeds without a live shape-search provider.
assertions: Contains API Gateway, Lambda, SQS, and DynamoDB components
The workflow does not require `search_shape_catalog` to succeed
If exact icons are unavailable, semantic/icon fallback still produces a valid offline bundle
The response does not claim a single live MCP implementation is required
The resulting artifact remains editable through `.drawio` plus sidecars

evals.prompt: Create a microservices architecture with API gateway, auth service, order service, event bus, and PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: A microservices diagram with 5 components and appropriate connectors.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue or similar engineering theme
Contains all 5 specified components
Database node uses database semantic type
Service-to-database edges use data connector type
Edge quality: no overlapping arrows, clear routing

evals.prompt: Create a network topology with firewall, core switch, access switch, and wireless APs. Include interface or VLAN labels on the links.
evals.expected_output: A network topology diagram with hierarchical layout showing 4 network components and labeled links.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue or similar engineering theme
Contains firewall, core switch, access switch, and AP nodes
Layout shows clear network hierarchy
Derived link labels include interface, VLAN, bandwidth, or link type metadata
Edge quality: orthogonal routing, no crossings where avoidable
meta.profile is set to engineering-review

evals.prompt: Create an AWS VPC topology with internet gateway, application load balancer, two EC2 app servers, and a private RDS subnet. Label the private links.
evals.expected_output: An engineering review topology with AWS icons and labeled private links.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue or similar engineering theme
Contains internet gateway, load balancer, app servers, and RDS/private subnet nodes
Provider icons are used for AWS infrastructure where requested
Derived link labels include listener/interface or private-link metadata
meta.profile is set to engineering-review

evals.prompt: Create a horizontal tech-blue login flowchart with Start, Login Form, Validate, Success, and Error.
evals.expected_output: A simple horizontal flowchart with 5 nodes in tech-blue theme.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue theme
Contains exactly 5 nodes: Start, Login Form, Validate, Success, Error
Layout direction is horizontal
Terminal nodes use terminal semantic type
Decision/branch is present for validate step

evals.prompt: Create a dense architecture diagram with shared event flows, two databases, and cross-service dependencies.
evals.expected_output: A complex architecture diagram that passes edge-quality validation.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue or similar engineering theme
Contains at least two database nodes
Event flows use data or optional connector types
Edge quality: no corner connection points, no shared face slots
Edge quality: last segment >= 30px, labels offset from edges
meta.profile is set to engineering-review
The diagram completes without depending on live incremental edit capabilities

evals.prompt: Create a paper figure for a training pipeline including the loss formula $$L = -\sum y_i \log(\hat{y}_i)$$.
evals.expected_output: An academic-themed training pipeline diagram with LaTeX formula rendering.
assertions: Output uses academic or academic-color theme
Contains a formula node with the loss function
LaTeX formula is properly formatted
SVG export preserves formula readability
meta.profile is set to academic-paper

evals.prompt: Replicate the uploaded approval-flow image. Keep the warm orange fills, brown connectors, and cream background instead of remapping everything to tech-blue.
evals.expected_output: A replicated spec that preserves the warm source palette with explicit node, edge, and module color overrides.
assertions: meta.source is set to replicated
meta.replication.colorMode is preserve-original
meta.replication includes a background color and extracted palette entries
High-confidence nodes, edges, and modules use explicit hex style overrides
The output does not collapse back to only default tech-blue palette values

evals.prompt: Recreate the uploaded architecture image and preserve its blue-green brand palette. Keep semantic shapes, but do not normalize colors to the default theme.
evals.expected_output: A replicated architecture diagram whose dominant colors stay close to the source brand palette.
assertions: meta.source is set to replicated
meta.replication.palette captures multiple extracted colors with confidence
Node styles contain explicit brand-blue and brand-green fills or strokes
Connector colors are preserved when clearly visible in the source
Low-confidence elements may fall back to theme tokens instead of inventing colors

evals.prompt: Replicate the uploaded paper figure. Keep the grayscale structure and its single accent highlight while remaining publication-safe.
evals.expected_output: A replicated academic figure with grayscale-safe structure and one preserved emphasis color.
assertions: meta.source is set to replicated
meta.replication.colorMode is preserve-original unless the user requests theme-first
The canvas and neutral elements remain grayscale-safe
Only the source accent color is preserved as an explicit override
The output stays suitable for academic-paper review

evals.prompt: Import the uploaded .drawio file into YAML spec and write sidecars.
evals.expected_output: A YAML spec bundle (.spec.yaml + .arch.json) that preserves the module, node labels, and basic connector semantics from the .drawio input.
assertions: Output spec contains meta.source: edited
Output spec contains module label Backend
Output spec contains nodes labeled API and DB
Node positions are present (x/y) on an 8px grid
Edge from API to DB imports as dashed/data connector type (or equivalent typed connector)

evals.prompt: Import the uploaded compressed .drawio file into YAML spec and write sidecars.
evals.expected_output: A YAML spec bundle imported from a compressed <diagram> payload that preserves modules, nodes, and connectors.
assertions: Importer decodes compressed diagram content into mxGraphModel XML
Output spec contains nodes labeled API and DB
Output spec contains module label Backend
Edge semantics (dashed/data) are preserved into typed connector fields

evals.prompt: Import the uploaded .drawio file into YAML spec, rename API to Auth, and export as SVG.
evals.expected_output: A regenerated SVG (and sidecars) reflecting the edited label with a validated structure.
assertions: The resulting spec and export rename API -> Auth
Output SVG is generated and is readable
Validation passes with --validate and paper-grade strict warnings when requested
The workflow remains valid without assuming a live session

evals.prompt: Edit the uploaded .drawio file offline: import it into YAML sidecars, rename API to Auth Service, and regenerate the bundle and SVG without assuming any live browser session.
evals.expected_output: An offline-imported spec bundle and regenerated export that prove the edit path does not depend on live incremental editing.
assertions: The `.drawio` input is imported into `.spec.yaml` and `.arch.json` before regeneration
The edited bundle renames API -> Auth Service
A regenerated SVG or drawio artifact is produced from the offline bundle
The task does not require `read_diagram_xml` or `patch_diagram_cells` to succeed
The offline sidecar bundle remains the canonical editable source

evals.prompt:
Convert this Mermaid flowchart to a draw.io diagram:

flowchart LR
  A[User Request] --> B[API Gateway]
  B --> C[Auth Service]
  B --> D[Order Service]
  C --> E[(User DB)]
  D --> F[(Order DB)]
evals.expected_output: A tech-blue themed diagram with 6 nodes converted from Mermaid input.
assertions: Output uses tech-blue theme
Contains all 6 nodes from Mermaid input
Database nodes use database semantic type
Edges preserve connectivity from Mermaid source
YAML spec is generated as intermediate representation

evals.prompt:
Convert this CSV into an org chart diagram:

name,parent,type
CEO,,user
VP Engineering,CEO,service
VP Product,CEO,service
Backend Lead,VP Engineering,service
Frontend Lead,VP Engineering,service
PM Lead,VP Product,service
evals.expected_output: A vertical org chart with 6 nodes showing hierarchy.
assertions: Output uses vertical layout
Contains all 6 org chart nodes
CEO node is a user type
Parent-child edges are correctly generated
Hierarchy is visually clear

evals.prompt: Create a neural network architecture diagram showing: Input Layer → Conv2D → BatchNorm → MaxPool → Attention Block → Dense Layer → Output, using academic theme.
evals.expected_output: An academic-themed neural network diagram using deep learning semantic node types.
assertions: Output uses academic or academic-color theme
Conv2D node uses conv semantic type
BatchNorm node uses norm semantic type
MaxPool node uses pool semantic type
Attention Block uses attention semantic type
Dense Layer uses matrix semantic type

evals.prompt: Create a microservices overview with API Gateway, User Service, Order Service, and PostgreSQL DB using dark theme for a presentation.
evals.expected_output: A dark-themed microservices diagram suitable for slides.
assertions: Output uses dark theme
Background color is dark (#0F172A or similar)
Contains all 4 specified components
Database node uses database semantic type
Node text colors provide sufficient contrast against dark background
